query: Academy Awards Board of Governors 2026-2027 elected members list branches

{"query":"Academy Awards Board of Governors 2026-2027 elected members list branches","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1406,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"http://press.oscars.org/news/academy-elects-2026-2027-board-governors","title":"THE ACADEMY ELECTS 2026-2027 BOARD OF GOVERNORS | Academy Press Office","content":"As of the 2026-2027 Board year, all 19 Academy branches will each have three branch-elected governors, adding additional governors to the Animation, Production and Technology and Short Films Branches."},{"url":"https://www.indiewire.com/awards/industry/guillermo-del-toro-academy-board-of-governors-2026-2027-1235200273/","title":"The Academy Board of Governors 2026-2027: Guillermo del Toro and More","content":"The Academy Board of Governors for 2026-2027 include first-time members like Oscar winner Guillermo del Toro and filmmaker David Leitch, who successfully advocated for the Stunt Design Oscar.
Daily Headlines covering Film, TV and more."},{"url":"https://www.hollywoodreporter.com/movies/movie-news/oscars-board-of-governors-guillermo-del-toro-kris-bowers-1236622103/","title":"Oscars Board of Governors 2026-2027: Guillermo del Toro, Kris Bowers","content":"Rookie governors will include three-time Oscar winner Guillermo del Toro (directors branch), who succeeds Ava DuVernay (mandatory two-year hiatus); composer and Oscar-winning short filmmaker Kris Bowers (music branch), who defeated Richard Gibbs (the sole incumbent who sought re-election and lost); and Oscar-nominated La La Land producer Fred Berger (producers), who succeeds Lynette Howell Taylor (mandatory two-year hiatus)."},{"url":"https://www.amnewscurtainraiser.com/2026/06/academy-elects-2026-27-board-of.html","title":"Academy Elects 2026-27 Board of Governors, Expanding Representation Across Branches - AmNews Curtain Raiser","content":"The Academy of Motion Picture Arts and Sciences has elected its 2026-27 Board of Governors, ushering in a new term that reflects a recent structural change intended to create equal representation across all 19 Academy branches.
Returning to the board after a hiatus are Bonnie Arnold of the Animation Branch, Bernard Telsey of the Casting Directors Branch, Roger Ross Williams of the Documentary Branch, Bob Rogers of the Short Films Branch, and Paul Debevec of the Visual Effects Branch.
The Academy, which has a global membership of more than 11,000 film artists and industry leaders, oversees the Oscars, Governors Awards, and Scientific and Technical Awards."},{"url":"https://variety.com/2026/film/awards/oscars-academy-board-governors-2026-2027-guillermo-del-toro-1236781704/","title":"Oscars Board of Governors Adds Guillermo del Toro, Kris Bowers","content":"The new board is comprised of a mixture of new faces to the board, like director Guillermo del Toro and composer Kris Bowers, returning incumbents like actor Lou Diamond Phillips and executive Hannah Minghella, and familiar names rejoining after a hiatus, including animation and documentary legends Bonnie Arnold and Roger Ross Williams.
Stoeckinger (Sound Branch) and Dana Stevens (Writers Branch).
Newly elected to the board for the first time are Oscar-winning director Guillermo del Toro (Directors Branch), Michael Goi (Cinematographers Branch), Anne Goursaud (Film Editors Branch), Patricia Dehaney (Makeup Artists and Hairstylists Branch), composer Kris Bowers (Music Branch), Fred Berger (Producers Branch), Vic Armstrong (Production and Technology Branch), David Leitch (Production and Technology Branch) and Kim Magnusson (Short Films Branch).
Because of the expansion, several governors drew shortened terms.
They join continuing governors Pam Abdy, Haifaa al-Mansour, Lesley Barber, K.K.
As a result of the election, the board is composed of 47% women and 32% members from underrepresented communities, based on self-reporting.
The board recently announced its honorees for the 17th Governors Awards."}]}
